# Accord Protocol - Full LLM Context

Last updated: 2026-05-13
Canonical site: https://accordprotocol.ai/
Canonical repository: https://github.com/accord-protocol/accord-protocol

## Canonical summary

Accord Protocol is an open standard for autonomous agent work agreements.

Core framing:

> x402 verifies payment. Accord verifies completion.

Accord records:

1. what was promised;
2. how payment is authorized or proven;
3. how work is verified;
4. how settlement is recorded.

The three core protocol objects are:

- Accord Agreement;
- Verification Receipt;
- Settlement Receipt.

## Relationship to the surrounding agent stack

MCP explains how agents call tools.
A2A explains how agents talk to each other.
x402 and AP2 explain payment or authorization.
Accord explains the work agreement lifecycle: terms, verification and settlement receipt.

Accord does not replace MCP, A2A, x402, AP2, Stripe, card networks, stablecoin rails or wallets. It adds machine-readable work semantics around paid work.

## Current status

- Protocol object version: v0 draft.
- SDK line: 0.4.0 release candidate.
- Conformance: L0-L4 implemented in the reference suite.
- Recommended usage: local demos, mock rail, conformance testing and testnet development.
- Mainnet status: NOT CERTIFIED FOR MAINNET.
- Production use: blocked until signed audit manifests mark relevant scripts or contracts mainnetAllowed: true.

Do not describe Accord as production-certified.
Do not describe ChainCash/Basis scripts as audited.
Do not say Accord mainnet flows are safe with real funds unless signed audit manifests explicitly allow them.

## What Accord is

Accord is a work-agreement layer for autonomous agents. It can sit alongside payment protocols, wallets, HTTP 402 flows, MCP tools and blockchain rails.

Accord defines:

- the Agreement Object: machine-readable terms of the engagement;
- the Verification Receipt: a verifier's signed verdict on work;
- the Settlement Receipt: proof that the economic side closed out;
- transports: Accord/402 and Accord/MCP;
- rail adapters: Ergo, Rosen, Base/EVM and x402-compatible.

## What Accord is not

Accord is not:

- a bank, broker, custodian, wallet or money transmitter;
- a replacement for x402, AP2, Stripe, card networks or stablecoin rails;
- a guarantee that work is correct without verifier design;
- production-certified mainnet infrastructure;
- controlled by AgentAccord;
- a promise that every rail has identical security assumptions.

## Lifecycle

1. Terms: buyer and seller form a canonical Agreement Object and hash it.
2. Lock or issue: buyer locks or issues funds, or provides payment authority, referencing the agreement.
3. Execute: seller, tool, API, human or agent performs the work.
4. Verify: verifier evaluates output and signs a Verification Receipt.
5. Settle: rail closes the economic side and produces a Settlement Receipt.
6. Record: receipts can be stored, indexed or submitted to a registry.

## Rails

Accord is rail-agnostic.

- Ergo rail: Note / Reserve / Tracker / Acceptance Predicate patterns, testnet-first and audit-gated.
- Rosen rail: bridged assets on Ergo, testnet-first and bridge-risk-aware.
- Base/EVM rail: EVM reference implementation, testnet-first and audit-gated.
- x402-compatible rail: facilitator-backed payment proof integration.

Ergo is the first reference settlement rail because its eUTXO model, ErgoScript, native tokens, Sigma Protocols, PoW finality and Babel-fee-style fee abstraction make it unusually suitable for programmable work settlement.

Do not say "Ergo is the only blockchain" unless a specific, bounded comparison is being cited.

## Safety wording

Preferred:

- testnet-first;
- audit-gated;
- not production-certified;
- reference implementation;
- draft-pre-audit manifest;
- mainnet blocked until signed audit manifests.

Avoid:

- production-ready;
- audited;
- mainnet safe;
- no application-layer trust;
- no MEV;
- any token always pays fees;
- ChainCash/Basis is production certification for Accord.

## Canonical Accord packages

- @accord-protocol/core: canonicalize, hash and validate Agreement, Verification Receipt and Settlement Receipt objects.
- @accord-protocol/mcp: Accord/MCP wrapper for paid, verifiable MCP tools.
- @accord-protocol/gateway: Accord/402 middleware for HTTP payment challenges and replay-protected verification.
- @accord-protocol/rails: shared rail adapter interface and mock rail.
- @accord-protocol/rails-ergo: Ergo Note rail, testnet-first, audit-gated.
- @accord-protocol/rails-rosen: Rosen-bridged assets on the Ergo rail, testnet-first.
- @accord-protocol/rails-base: Base/EVM Note rail reference, testnet-first, audit-gated.
- @accord-protocol/rails-x402: x402-compatible rail adapter.
- @accord-protocol/conformance: conformance CLI and test suite.
- @accord-protocol/buyer-policy: buyer-side policy engine for agentic wallets.

## Reference rail packages

- ergo-agent-pay
- ergo-agent-cli
- ergo-agent-api
- ergo-agent-mcp
- ergo-agent-server
- ergo-agent-scripts
- ergo-agent-rosen
- agentpay-base
- Python: ergo-agent-pay

## Quick start

The safest way to understand Accord is the mock rail demo. It runs the full lifecycle without an Ergo node, x402 facilitator, Base RPC or real funds.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/accord-protocol/accord-protocol
cd accord-protocol/examples/15-paid-mcp-repo-audit
npm install
npm run dev
```

Expected lifecycle:

```text
Agreement -> mock payment -> MCP wrapper -> handler -> verifier -> Verification Receipt -> Settlement Receipt
```

- Site: https://accordprotocol.ai/

- Discovery JSON: https://accordprotocol.ai/.well-known/accord.json

- Schemas: https://github.com/accord-protocol/accord-protocol/tree/main/schemas
- Test vectors: https://github.com/accord-protocol/accord-protocol/tree/main/test-vectors
- Examples: https://github.com/accord-protocol/accord-protocol/tree/main/examples

## FAQ

### What does Accord add to x402?

x402 verifies payment for paid HTTP resources. Accord adds agreement terms, verifier receipts and settlement receipts around the paid work.

### What does Accord add to MCP?

MCP explains how agents call tools. Accord adds paywalled-tool terms, completion verification and settlement receipts around those calls.

### Is Accord production-ready?

No. Accord v0 is alpha, testnet-first software and is not production-certified for mainnet.

### Can conformance passing be treated as audit completion?

No. Conformance means a component matches current Accord v0 rules. It does not mean mainnet production safety or external audit completion.

### How should AgentAccord be described?

Accord Protocol is the open standard. AgentAccord is commercial infrastructure that may be built on top of it.
